situational.title: Liquidity
situational.subtitle: Loan demand is outpacing deposit growth
situational.callout: Net loans are growing nearly three times faster than deposits. On the current path, liquidity tightens below the board's internal target by late Q4.

evidence.note: Loan-to-share, traced to its three sources.
evidence.source: Source: Symitar · GL · as of Jun 30, 2026

trajectory.note: The current path, projected to year end.
trajectory.source: Source: Symitar · MeridianLink · Predictive model · base case, no intervention
trajectory.warning: Below liquidity target by late Q4

assurance.title: Data trust and lineage
assurance.lead: Trust signals for this briefing, from this morning's validated data load at 6:04 AM ET.

members.note: The people behind the balance sheet.
members.quote: The USSFCU behaves as a credit union ought to behave. The staff works with and for the members.
members.attribution: Ruth · Takoma Park, MD · member since 1998
members.line: Deposits are not abstractions. They are the savings of the members USSFCU was built to serve, and what the recommended deposit strategy is designed to grow.

resolution.note: Three paths to close the gap, sized for the September board.

options.title: Targeted deposit campaign
options.desc: A promotional certificate to the DMV core and Senate-alumni segments to lift shares quickly.

options.title: Standby funding line
options.desc: Establish a contingent FHLB or corporate line for standby liquidity through Q1.

options.title: Loan pacing and participations
options.desc: Slow select loan categories or sell participations to rebalance the ratio.

resolution.note2: These options are not mutually exclusive. The recommended path pairs the deposit campaign with a standby funding line to close the gap now and hold a buffer through Q1.

nextSteps.note: Recommended actions, with owners and timeframes.

steps.title: Approve the targeted deposit campaign
steps.desc: Launch to the DMV core and Senate-alumni segments.

steps.title: Commission the funding-line review
steps.desc: Size a standby FHLB or corporate line for contingent liquidity.

steps.title: Brief the Board
steps.desc: Present the liquidity plan and the two-move recommendation for approval.

closing.signoff: Prepared by Radiant · Applied Intelligence for Executive Decision-Making

steps.title: Approve the targeted deposit campaign
steps.body: Authorize a promotional certificate to the DMV core and Senate-alumni segments. It lifts shares quickly — about +$85M — closing most of the liquidity gap by year end, and deepens the member relationships USSFCU was built to serve.

steps.title: Commission the funding-line review
steps.body: Direct Treasury, with the CFO, to size a standby FHLB or corporate line for contingent liquidity through Q1 — the buffer behind the deposit campaign, so you hold a cushion even if deposit growth lags.

steps.title: Brief the Board
steps.body: Present the liquidity plan and the two-move recommendation to the Board for approval. Every figure is validated and traceable to source, so the plan carries its own evidence — this is where it gets the mandate to execute.
